---
title: "Assignment 9"
author: "Sophie Meade"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)#thisone

library(sf)
library(tidyverse)
library(spdep) # Creating spatial weights
library(knitr)

chicago <- st_read("Chicago_2020_sdoh.shp", quiet = TRUE)
chicago <- st_transform(chicago, 32614)  # UTM Zone 14N
st_crs(chicago)

class(chicago)

#t-test
mean(chicago$MEDINC) #56199.94

t.test(chicago$MEDINC, mu = 83730)

#How does the mean median income in Chicago differ from the national avergae?

nb_queen <- poly2nb(chicago, queen = TRUE)
summary(nb_queen)

coords <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(chicago))

{
plot(st_geometry(chicago), border = "gray", main = "Queen Contiguity Neighbors")
plot(nb_queen, coords = coords, add = TRUE, col = "red", lwd = 1)
}

# All neighbors within 20km (20000 meters)
nb_20km <- dnearneigh(coords, 0, 20000)

summary(nb_20km)

# Add to data


islands_queen <- which(card(nb_queen) == 0)

if (length(islands_queen) > 0) {
  cat("WARNING: Found", length(islands_queen), "islands (no neighbors):\n")
  print(chicago$mun_name[islands_queen])
} else {
  cat("✓ No islands found - all municipalities have at least one neighbor\n")
}

coords <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(chicago))

# Calculate all pairwise distances
k1 <- knn2nb(knearneigh(coords, k = 1))
critical_threshold <- max(unlist(nbdists(k1, coords)))

cat("Maximum distance to nearest neighbor:", round(critical_threshold/1000, 2), "km\n") #7.45
cat("This is the MINIMUM distance needed to ensure no islands\n")

# All neighbors within 20km (20000 meters)
nb_8km <- dnearneigh(coords, 0, 8000)

summary(nb_8km)

# Add to data
chicago$n_neighbors_8km <- card(nb_8km)

islands_distance <- which(card(nb_8km) == 0)

if (length(islands_distance) > 0) {
  cat("WARNING: Found", length(islands_distance), "islands (no neighbors):\n")
  print(chicago$mun_name[islands_distance])
} else {
  cat("✓ No islands found - all municipalities have at least one neighbor\n")
} #no islands for distance!

knn5 <- knn2nb(knearneigh(coords, k = 5))

summary(knn5)

# Verify: everyone has exactly 5 neighbors
table(card(knn5))

listw_queen <- nb2listw(nb_queen, style = "W")
listw_rook <- nb2listw(nb_rook, style = "W")
listw_8km <- nb2listw(nb_8km, style = "W")
listw_knn5 <- nb2listw(knn5, style = "W")

summary(listw_queen)
summary(listw_rook)
summary(listw_8km)
summary(listw_knn5) #ask about this; where is number of neighbors?

summary(knn5)

islands <- which(card(listw_queen) == 0)

if (length(islands) > 0) {
  cat("WARNING: Found", length(islands), "islands (no neighbors):\n")
  print(chicago$mun_name[islands])
} else {
  cat("✓ No islands found - all municipalities have at least one neighbor\n")
}

#OKAY WEIGHTS
weights_comparison <- tibble(
  weight_type = c("Queen Contiguity", "Rook Contiguity", 
                  "8km Distance", "5-Nearest Neighbors"),
  avg_neighbors = c(
    mean(card(nb_queen)),
    mean(card(nb_rook)),
    mean(card(nb_8km)),
    5
  ),
  min_neighbors = c(
    min(card(nb_queen)),
    min(card(nb_rook)),
    min(card(nb_8km)),
    5
  ),
  max_neighbors = c(
    max(card(nb_queen)),
    max(card(nb_rook)),
    max(card(nb_8km)),
    5
  ),
  is_symmetric = c(
    is.symmetric.nb(nb_queen),
    is.symmetric.nb(nb_rook),
    is.symmetric.nb(nb_8km),
    is.symmetric.nb(knn5)
  ),
  
  S1 = c(
    summary(listw_queen)$S1,
    summary(listw_rook)$S1,
    summary(listw_8km)$S1,
    summary(listw_knn5)$S1
  ),
  
  S2 = c(
    summary(listw_queen)$S2,
    summary(listw_rook)$S2,
    summary(listw_8km)$S2,
    summary(listw_knn5)$S2
  )
)


kable(weights_comparison, digits = 2,
      col.names = c("Weight Type", "Mean Neighbors", "Min Neighbors", 
                    "Max Neighbors", "Symmetric"))

ncol(weights_comparison)




```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}

mean(chicago$MEDINC) #56199.94
t.test(chicago$MEDINC, mu = 83730)

```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

{
plot(st_geometry(chicago), border = "gray", main = "Queen Contiguity Neighbors")
plot(nb_queen, coords = coords, add = TRUE, col = "red", lwd = 1)
}

{
plot(st_geometry(chicago), border = "gray", main = "Rook Contiguity Neighbors")
plot(nb_rook, coords = coords, add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1)
}

kable(weights_comparison, digits = 2,
      col.names = c("Weight Type", "Mean Neighbors", "Min Neighbors", 
                    "Max Neighbors", "Symmetric"))

hist(card(nb_queen), main = "Queen", xlab = "# Neighbors", col = "blue")
hist(card(nb_rook), main = "Rook", xlab = "# Neighbors", col = "green")
hist(card(nb_8km), main = "8km Distance", xlab = "# Neighbors", col = "red")


```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
